title: Extra Hours, Lost in Smoke
theme: A small summer complaint told through a sky that keeps getting edited down.
style_anchor: observational urban realism with muted colors, crisp edges, and dry documentary timing
accent: hazy amber light with soot-gray shadows

protagonist.description: A tired city resident in a wrinkled short-sleeve shirt, practical sneakers, and a face that has already accepted too many weather updates.
protagonist.silhouette: slouched figure with a coffee-cup shoulder and a hand raised to squint at the sky

scenes.prompt:
Human-Readable house style: a mostly monochrome, hand-drawn editorial webcomic. The recurring protagonist is a minimalist stick figure with a round head, dot eyes, and a consistent silhouette. Backgrounds are detailed and grounded; characters remain deliberately simple. Use clean black ink, restrained hatching, and at most one warm orange accent color. Dialogue is short, legible, and hand-lettered in plain speech bubbles. The humor is dry, observational, technically aware, and never explained.

observational urban realism with muted colors, crisp edges, and dry documentary timing

Render one self-contained cinematic comic scene, not a multi-panel page. Camera: wide.

Location: community park lawn with a view of the skyline. Scene: A neighborhood park at late afternoon: the sun is still high enough to be annoying, yet the light looks filtered through dirty glass. People linger on benches, all looking upward like they expected a better performance from the day.

Recurring protagonist (protagonist_01): A tired city resident in a wrinkled short-sleeve shirt, practical sneakers, and a face that has already accepted too many weather updates. Silhouette: slouched figure with a coffee-cup shoulder and a hand raised to squint at the sky

Keep the protagonist's proportions, face, and silhouette identical across every scene. Accent color: hazy amber light with soot-gray shadows.

Visible prop continuity:
- park_bench_01: bench edge; A weathered wooden bench with a metal armrest warmed unevenly by the sun.; stationary park fixture; appears in this scene only
- smoke_haze_01: haze layer; A visible amber-gray haze softening the skyline and turning the sunlight into a reluctant suggestion.; environmental condition; persists across all scenes as the same smoky weather pattern

World prop reference (IDs are metadata; never print an ID in the artwork):
park_bench_01: bench edge; A weathered wooden bench with a metal armrest warmed unevenly by the sun.; continuity: stationary park fixture; appears in this scene only
smoke_haze_01: haze layer; A visible amber-gray haze softening the skyline and turning the sunlight into a reluctant suggestion.; continuity: environmental condition; persists across all scenes as the same smoky weather pattern
phone_01: time display; A scratched phone showing the late-afternoon hour and an aggressively bright weather app.; continuity: held by protagonist in this scene only
lemonade_cart_01: closed cart latch; A faded lemonade cart with its metal shutter pulled halfway down and a handwritten sign that says ‘back when the air improves.’; continuity: background prop unique to this scene
folding_chair_01: chair creak; A mismatched folding chair with one leg wrapped in bright tape.; continuity: rooftop seating; not reused elsewhere
sun_disk_01: sun disk; A pale orange sun reduced to a flattened coin by the haze, still bright enough to refuse dignity.; continuity: same sun event as scenes 1, 2, 4, 5, and 6
thermometer_01: temperature gauge; An old glass thermometer stuck to the window, reading a little too warmly for the mood.; continuity: window fixture unique to this scene
houseplant_01: leaf shine; A dust-speckled pothos leaning toward the window with one leaf curled like it has a complaint pending.; continuity: indoor plant unique to this scene
whiteboard_01: marker note; A slightly smudged whiteboard covered in optimistic plans, crossed-out doodles of clouds, and one very determined sun sketch.; continuity: meeting-room fixture unique to this scene
dry_erase_marker_01: marker cap; A blue dry-erase marker left uncapped, staining one fingertip and everyone’s sense of preparedness.; continuity: used for the whiteboard in this scene only
streetlight_01: lamp glow; A streetlight flickering on before it should have to, because the day gave up early.; continuity: nightfall fixture unique to this scene
paper_cup_01: coffee cup; A plain paper cup with a lid, nearly empty and warm enough to be comforting by accident.; continuity: held by a neighbor in this scene only

Dialogue to reproduce exactly and legibly:
- "We get a few extra hours of sun and somehow the sky still comes with a disclaimer."
- "I was promised summer. This feels like a beta version."

Use detailed environmental storytelling and readable prop labels. Do not add a title, scene number, caption explaining the joke, or metadata IDs.

scenes.location: community park lawn with a view of the skyline
dialogue: "We get a few extra hours of sun and somehow the sky still comes with a disclaimer."
"I was promised summer. This feels like a beta version."

props.label: bench edge
props.description: A weathered wooden bench with a metal armrest warmed unevenly by the sun.
props.continuity: stationary park fixture; appears in this scene only
props.interaction_detail: The paint has worn smooth exactly where tired people rest their hands while making the same complaint.

props.label: haze layer
props.description: A visible amber-gray haze softening the skyline and turning the sunlight into a reluctant suggestion.
props.continuity: environmental condition; persists across all scenes as the same smoky weather pattern
props.interaction_detail: Looking through it makes distant trees seem painted in the wrong layer, as if the sky skipped a step.
